name: Granulator
description: This algorithm is an implementation of the original Granulator algorithm on the disting EX. It implements a granular synthesis engine, taking as its source material either live audio input or audio loaded from the MicroSD card. Live audio can be recorded into a buffer or streamed continuously. Granular synthesis works by playing many short snippets of sound, or 'grains', typically of the order of 100ms in length. Often various properties of the grains (e.g. their timing, length, pitch, stereo panning etc.) are randomised to some extent. In this algorithm, the creation ('spawning') of grains is controlled by 'notes'. Notes control when grain clouds begin and end, and affect other features e.g. the grain pitch. Notes can be played via CV/gate pairs, or MIDI, or I2C. The algorithm also offers three 'drone' voices, which can simply be enabled via the usual parameter interface. When using the algorithm as an audio processing effect you're likely to just enable one or more of these drones and leave them on while manipulating other grain parameters.
gui_description: The display shows a waveform representation of the audio buffer, on which are superimposed dots indicating the currently playing grains. The two dotted lines represent the 'Size mean' (by their separation) and the 'Delay mean' (by their position within the buffer). On the right of the screen you can see the current buffer size, the current delay, and the number of grains currently playing.

parameters.name: Input gain
parameters.description: Gain applied to the audio being recorded (does not affect the dry signal).

parameters.name: Dry gain
parameters.description: Level of the input signal in the output mix.

parameters.name: Granulator gain
parameters.description: Level of the granulator signal in the output mix.

parameters.name: Input feedback
parameters.description: The amount of feedback to apply around the audio buffer itself when recording (resulting in an echo effect on the input material, with a delay time equal to the buffer size).

parameters.name: Main feedback
parameters.description: The amount of the granulator output to feed back into the audio buffer when recording.

parameters.name: Normalize
parameters.description: If enabled, the overall volume of the grain cloud is lowered according to how many grains are active.

parameters.name: Record
parameters.description: Enables recording into the buffer.

parameters.name: Buffer size
parameters.description: The audio buffer size in milliseconds.

parameters.name: Record fade
parameters.description: The duration of the fade applied when starting and stopping recording, to avoid clicks.

parameters.name: Shape
parameters.description: The grain envelope/window shape.

parameters.name: Spawn mode
parameters.description: How grains are spawned.

parameters.name: Rate mean
parameters.description: The average time between new grains being spawned.

parameters.name: Rate spread
parameters.description: The amount of variation in the spawn rate, expressed as a percentage of 'Rate mean'.

parameters.name: Size mean
parameters.description: The average grain size. '0' has a special meaning – see below.

parameters.name: Size spread
parameters.description: The amount of variation in grain size, expressed as a percentage of 'Size mean'.

parameters.name: Pitch mean
parameters.description: The average grain pitch shift (in semitones). This is added to the pitch shift determined by the note's pitch CV.

parameters.name: Pitch spread
parameters.description: The amount of variation in grain pitch shift.

parameters.name: Pan mean
parameters.description: The average grain pan position.

parameters.name: Pan spread
parameters.description: The amount of variation in grain pan.

parameters.name: Delay mean
parameters.description: The average grain delay (equivalently, the position in the audio buffer), expressed as a percentage of the buffer size.

parameters.name: Delay spread
parameters.description: The amount of variation in grain delay, expressed as a percentage of the buffer size.

parameters.name: Reverse
parameters.description: Sets the probability that a grain will be played backwards.

parameters.name: Grain limit
parameters.description: Imposes an arbitrary limit on the number of simultaneous grains.

parameters.name: Natural pitch
parameters.description: Sets the natural pitch of the audio i.e. the MIDI note number that will play back the audio at the same pitch at which it was recorded.

parameters.name: Pitch quantize
parameters.description: Quantizes the random pitch deviation (the sum of the 'Pitch mean' and 'Pitch spread') to musical intervals.

parameters.name: Opacity
parameters.description: The 'opacity' of a note, which is the percentage of grains that would normally make up the note that actually sound.

parameters.name: Grain position
parameters.description: Sets how the grain position is computed relative to the position set by the Delay mean/spread parameters. The options are 'Centred on delay' and 'Start at delay'.

parameters.name: LFO depth
parameters.description: The depth of the LFO that affects the grain delay, expressed as a percentage of the buffer size.

parameters.name: LFO speed
parameters.description: The speed of the grain delay LFO. This is scaled relative to the buffer size – at the default value of 196 the LFO will cause the 'play head' (to use a tape metaphor) to advance at 1x speed.

parameters.name: LFO shape
parameters.description: Sets the LFO shape. The options are 'Triangle', 'Ramp up', and 'Ramp down'.

parameters.name: Attack time
parameters.description: The envelope attack time, from 100ms to 30s with an exponential scaling.

parameters.name: Release time
parameters.description: The envelope release time, from 100ms to 30s with an exponential scaling.

parameters.name: Env -> opacity
parameters.description: The amount by which the note envelope affects the note opacity.

parameters.name: Env -> level
parameters.description: The amount by which the note envelope affects the note level (volume).

parameters.name: Veloc -> level
parameters.description: The amount by which the note velocity affects the note level (volume).

parameters.name: Veloc -> delay
parameters.description: The amount by which the note velocity affects the grain delay.

parameters.name: Pitch -> pitch
parameters.description: The amount by which the note pitch affects the grain pitch. Commonly this will either be 100% (normal pitch tracking) or 0% (the incoming pitch doesn't affect the grain pitch at all, but may still affect e.g. the grain delay).

parameters.name: Pitch -> delay
parameters.description: The amount by which the note pitch affects the grain delay.

parameters.name: Drone 1-3 pitch
parameters.description: The MIDI note number for the drone. Remember that 'Natural pitch' sets how these are interpreted. For the default Natural pitch of 48, done pitch 48 is the same pitch, drone pitch 36 is an octave down, and drone pitch 60 is an octave up.

parameters.name: Drone 1-3 enable
parameters.description: Enables (gates) the drone.

parameters.name: Drone 1-3 opacity
parameters.description: The opacity of the drone.

parameters.name: Drone 1-3 level
parameters.description: The level (volume) of the drone.

parameters.name: MIDI channel
parameters.description: The MIDI channel to listen on.

parameters.name: MPE channels
parameters.description: Controls how the algorithm will respond to MPE. See above.

parameters.name: I2C channel
parameters.description: Sets the I2C channel.

parameters.name: Bend range
parameters.description: The MIDI pitch bend range.

parameters.name: Delay unit
parameters.description: Determines whether the Delay mean and spread parameters work in terms of % or ms.

parameters.name: Output mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector as described above.

parameters.name: Delay mean sampled
parameters.description: If enabled, the Delay mean input is sampled once at the start of a note. Otherwise, it is sampled afresh as every grain spawns.

input_ports.name: Delay mean input
input_ports.description: The input bus to use to control the grain delay. A CV of 5V corresponds to 100% of the buffer size.

input_ports.name: Delay mean input
input_ports.description: The input bus to use to control the grain delay. A CV of 5V corresponds to 100% of the buffer size.
